raw_customer_language: The tub spout keeps dripping even when the fixture is off.
It has a hot knob, a middle knob, and a cold knob.
The middle knob sends the water up to the shower or back down to the tub.
I do not know the brand, but there is an access panel behind the shower.
Do you charge a diagnostic fee, and what does something like this usually cost?
reported_symptoms: tub spout keeps dripping when fixture is off
reported_fixture_details: middle knob changes flow between shower and tub
reported_policy_questions: usual cost for this kind of issue

ambiguities.reason: The source reports the symptom and fixture layout, but the internal repair cause remains unverified.
The customer directly states they do not know the brand.
The access panel is reported, but usability and exact access constraints still need human review.

missing_information: whether dripping is continuous or intermittent
whether there are additional access constraints

policy_questions.customer_language: Do you charge a diagnostic fee?
What does something like this usually cost?

review_recommendations: Review the possible service category before any operational use.
Keep the brand unknown until a human verifies it.
Confirm whether the access panel is usable for service planning.
Keep diagnostic fee and general cost questions separate from reported symptoms.
Treat the 1:00 PM statement as a customer preference only.

confidence.notes: This manual sample preserves source phrasing and visible uncertainty. It is a review aid only, not operational truth.
